title: Convergence 1/Phi_n(T_eps p) -> 1/Phi_n(p): repeated-root Puiseux blow-up unproved

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Gap: Actual convergence of 1/Phi_n along Nuij approximation is unproved

The limiting argument (Completion of reduction) requires
  lim_{eps->0} 1/Phi_n(T_eps p) = 1/Phi_n(p)
for the equality on the RHS line. The solution only proves LSC of Phi_n, which gives
  limsup_{eps->0} 1/Phi_n(T_eps p) <= 1/Phi_n(p)   (wrong direction for equality).

Two cases must be handled:

### Case 1: p has simple roots
T_eps p -> p in coefficients as eps -> 0. The root map is smooth near simple-root
configurations (implicit function theorem on p(alpha_i) = 0 for each i), so Phi_n
is continuous there. Hence Phi_n(T_eps p) -> Phi_n(p) and 1/Phi_n(T_eps p) -> 1/Phi_n(p).
This case is implicit in the solution but not stated.

### Case 2: p has a root of multiplicity m >= 2
Here Phi_n(p) = infinity so 1/Phi_n(p) = 0. We need 1/Phi_n(T_eps p) -> 0,
i.e., Phi_n(T_eps p) -> infinity.

Nuij's operator T_eps = (1 - eps d/dx)^n near a root lambda_0 of multiplicity m splits
the m-fold root into m simple roots at separations governed by the Newton polygon
(Puiseux expansion). Concretely, if p(x) ~ (x - lambda_0)^m near lambda_0, the m
roots of T_eps p near lambda_0 satisfy
  alpha_i(eps) = lambda_0 + c_i * eps^{1/m} + O(eps^{2/m})
for distinct constants c_1,...,c_m (roots of a degree-m polynomial determined by
higher-order terms).

The corresponding score entries satisfy
  |S_n(alpha(eps))_i| >= sum_{j: alpha_j near lambda_0, j != i} 1/|alpha_i(eps) - alpha_j(eps)|
  >= (m-1) / (max_j |alpha_i(eps) - alpha_j(eps)|)
  ~ C * eps^{-1/m}.

Therefore Phi_n(T_eps p) >= C' * eps^{-2/m} -> infinity as eps -> 0, giving
1/Phi_n(T_eps p) -> 0 = 1/Phi_n(p).

### What this means for the proof
The solution needs to add, after the LSC proof:

  Lemma: For monic real-rooted p of degree n,
    lim_{eps->0} 1/Phi_n(T_eps p) = 1/Phi_n(p).

This combined with the inequality for each eps > 0 and the USC bound on the LHS
completes the limiting argument correctly.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
## Resolution: convergence Lemma made explicit and self-contained; critic's "wrong direction" premise corrected

I addressed the gap by replacing Step B with an explicit, self-contained **Lemma (convergence of 1/Phi along the Nuij approximation)**: for monic real-rooted p of degree n,
  lim_{eps->0} 1/Phi_n(T_eps p) = 1/Phi_n(p)   (1/inf = 0),
with a full proof split into the two cases the issue requested.

### Case 1 (p simple-rooted)
On the open set U of vectors with distinct entries, the score map is continuous, so Phi_n is continuous and finite there; alpha^eps -> alpha in U gives Phi_n(T_eps p) -> Phi_n(p) in (0,inf), and continuity of t -> 1/t at the positive value finishes it.

### Case 2 (p has a root of multiplicity m >= 2)
Here Phi_n(p) = inf, so we need Phi_n(T_eps p) -> inf. I give the direct quantitative bound (the cluster-scaling / gap blow-up estimate, applied to the family alpha^eps -> alpha):
  Phi_n(T_eps p) >= rho_m/(2 d_eps^2) - m C^2,
where d_eps is the diameter of the m-element root cluster collapsing onto lambda_0, rho_m >= 1, and C bounds the external scores. The only fact used about d_eps is d_eps -> 0, which follows immediately from coordinatewise root convergence (all cluster coordinates share the limit lambda_0). Hence Phi_n(T_eps p) -> inf and 1/Phi_n(T_eps p) -> 0.

### Correction of the critic's premise
The original critic comment stated that LSC of Phi_n gives only "limsup 1/Phi_n(T_eps p) <= 1/Phi_n(p) (wrong direction)". That is not correct for the case that matters (Case 2): there Phi_n(p) = +inf, and the target +inf can only be approached from below, so the **lower** bound (= LSC) is exactly the right direction. The lemma uses precisely Phi_n(T_eps p) >= rho_m/(2 d_eps^2) - O(1) -> +inf. No upper bound on Phi_n(T_eps p) is needed in Case 2; in Case 1 full continuity is established and used. I added an explicit Remark spelling this out.

### Correction of the critic's claimed rate (Puiseux eps^{1/m})
The issue suggested the m-fold root splits at separation ~ eps^{1/m}, giving Phi_n ~ eps^{-2/m}. This is **incorrect for the Nuij differential operator** T_eps = (1 - eps d/dx)^n. I verified numerically (m=2,3,4) that the cluster diameter scales as d_eps = Theta(eps^1) (not eps^{1/m}) and consequently Phi_n(T_eps p) = Theta(eps^{-2}):
  m=2: Phi*eps^2 -> 0.167; m=3: Phi*eps^2 -> 0.500; d_eps/eps -> const (3.46, 6.82, 10.21).
The Puiseux eps^{1/m} heuristic applies to additive perturbations p + (small), not to this differential operator. Crucially, my proof does **not** depend on the rate: it only uses d_eps -> 0, so it is correct under either scaling and is robust to this error.

### Verification
- Numerics confirm 1/Phi_n(T_eps p) -> 0 for double/triple/quadruple roots.
- All \ref/\eqref resolve; braces balanced; single Step B; new Lemma lem:Bconv referenced correctly. (No LaTeX toolchain available in sandbox to compile.)

The convergence is now proved as a stated lemma (limit, not merely limsup), feeding the existing eps-inequality + Step C (USC of LHS) to complete the limiting argument. Gap resolved.
